deck_summary_one_sentence: 4th edition of Strategy& eReadiness Survey covering 18 countries and 12,500+ consumer interviews, segmenting consumers into EV Owners/Prospects/Sceptics, ranking countries on an eReadiness Index, and recommending actions for e-mobility players.

color_palette.notes: Same Strategy& red palette as EV Sales Review and Digital Auto Report — strongly suggests shared brand template across automotive practice.

chart_styling.notes: Consumer survey charts show consistent treatment across cluster comparisons (Owners/Prospects/Sceptics); use color encoding for cluster, gray for baseline.

layout_conventions.no_fly_zones_observed: Top-band for action titles. Bottom-band for 'eReadiness Report 2023' footer + page number. Survey methodology slides use standard 3-column comparison layout for cluster profiles.

slides.title_text: eReadiness 2023 Survey Report — Customer needs and recommended actions for e-mobility players
slides.main_message: Cover slide: eReadiness 2023 Survey Report — Customer needs and recommended actions for e-mobility players (4th edition, September 2023)

slides.title_text: Agenda
slides.main_message: Agenda: 01 Exec Summary, 02 Consumer viewpoints (EV Owners/Prospects/Sceptics), 03 eReadiness Index, 04 Recommendations on the way forward

slides.title_text: The 4th edition of the study provides updated perspectives on the short-term development of the e-mobility business in 18 markets
slides.main_message: About the study: 1st edition 4 countries, 4th edition 18 countries (2020-2023); private passenger car customers; BEV+PHEV; 12,500+ CAWI interviews Jan-May 2023

slides.title_text: eReadiness 2023 — Countries in scope (18 countries across the globe)
slides.main_message: 18 countries across 3 regions: NA (USA, Canada), EMEA (France, Germany, Italy, Norway, Poland, Spain, Switzerland, UK, UAE), APAC (Australia, China, Hong Kong, India, Japan, Singapore, Thailand)

slides.title_text: Executive Summary — section header
slides.main_message: Section divider: Executive Summary

slides.title_text: Key insights from the consumer research sample
slides.main_message: Exec summary: 30% intend to buy EV in next 2 years; EV Owners 6% (high-income middle-aged males urban); EV Prospects 61% (~20% lower income, 6 personas); Sceptics 31% (women, lower income, older); 20% online vehicle sales for premium; 60% interest in used EV but battery SoH uncertainty

slides.title_text: Consumer viewpoints — section header
slides.main_message: Section divider: Consumer viewpoints

slides.title_text: Consumers have been grouped into 3 main clusters within 3 regions: EV owners, EV prospects and EV sceptics
slides.main_message: Cluster methodology: EV owners (current), EV prospects (will buy in 5 years), EV sceptics (won't); n=12,816; each cluster has focus areas, outcomes, and descriptions

slides.title_text: EV owners are younger, wealthier and with greater access to private parking spaces compared to prospects and sceptics
slides.main_message: Cluster demographics: Owners €91k income / 43yrs / 53% male / 82% urban / 63% private parking; Prospects €74k / 42yrs / 51% male / 92% urban / 68% private parking; Sceptics €49k / 49yrs / 44% male / 94% urban / 60% private parking

slides.title_text: Current EV owners tend to use their car more often for commuting, and are more likely to combine it with other means of transportation
slides.main_message: Commuting profile by cluster: Owners 86% commute by car / 4.4 days / 23km / 60% multi-modal; Prospects 78% / 4.2 days / 28km / 52% multi-modal; Sceptics 60% / 3.2 days / 20km / 31% multi-modal

slides.title_text: EV owners commute primarily with their car and adopt more multi-modal solutions compared to sceptics
slides.main_message: Commuting modes detail and parking switch behavior across EV owner / prospect / sceptic clusters

slides.title_text: Mobility needs — Free time usage patterns
slides.main_message: Free time mobility patterns across clusters showing different usage of cars and alternative modes

slides.title_text: 02. Consumer viewpoints — EV Owners deep dive
slides.main_message: Section divider: deep dive into EV Owners (~40 slides covering owner journey, usage, charging, satisfaction)

slides.title_text: EV Owners — customer journey and usage habits (multiple slides aggregated)
slides.main_message: EV owner journey: trigger, research, purchase, ownership; usage habits — daily/weekly patterns, charging routines, satisfaction drivers

slides.title_text: EV Owners — Charging solutions and preferences
slides.main_message: Charging analysis: home AC charging dominant; public DC for long trips; willingness to pay for charging speed

slides.title_text: Charging location and availability are key areas of dissatisfaction for EV owners
slides.main_message: Charging point dissatisfaction: location availability, reliability, payment friction are top complaints; cross-country comparison

slides.title_text: EV Owners — Additional charging analyses (multiple slides aggregated)
slides.main_message: Charging cost analysis, public vs home preferences, charging time tolerance

slides.title_text: 02. Consumer viewpoints — EV Prospects deep dive
slides.main_message: Section divider: deep dive into EV Prospects (intention to buy in 5 years)

slides.title_text: EV Prospects — Key personas with distinctive traits
slides.main_message: 6 EV prospect personas identified; Tech Enthusiasts, Dreamers and Pragmatic drive ~70% of next 2 years demand — market shifting toward mass-market

slides.title_text: EV Prospects — Key reasons to buy (multiple slides aggregated)
slides.main_message: Top reasons for prospects to buy EV: cost of fuel/charging, environmental concerns, tech features, government incentives, range increase

slides.title_text: Charging duration, range and battery lifetime are the key barriers stopping EV prospects from purchasing
slides.main_message: Top barriers for EV prospects: charging duration, driving range, battery lifetime; charging time and range expectations charted

slides.title_text: 02. Consumer viewpoints — EV Sceptics deep dive
slides.main_message: Section divider: deep dive into EV Sceptics (reject EVs in next 5 years)

slides.title_text: EV Sceptics — Distinctive traits and main reasons for rejecting EVs
slides.main_message: Sceptic traits: predominantly women, lower income, 6 years older than prospects; main rejection reasons centered on price, charging infrastructure concerns, range anxiety

slides.title_text: 03. eReadiness Index — section header
slides.main_message: Section divider: eReadiness Index (Index methodology + country rankings)

slides.title_text: eReadiness Index — Methodology and weights
slides.main_message: Index methodology covering consumer demand, charging infrastructure, EV supply, government support; weighting and country scoring approach

slides.title_text: Europe eReadiness — Norway, Switzerland, Germany are most e-ready; Italy and Spain lag despite incentives
slides.main_message: Europe ranking: Norway / Switzerland / Germany top driven by mature charging infrastructure and high consumer demand; Italy and Spain lag despite generous government incentives

slides.title_text: APAC eReadiness — Hong Kong, China, Singapore lead; Australia ranks last
slides.main_message: APAC ranking: Hong Kong / China / Singapore lead with high demand + infrastructure; Australia is least e-ready across the entire 18-country panel

slides.title_text: North America eReadiness — USA and Canada profiles
slides.main_message: NA ranking and profile comparing USA and Canada across consumer demand, charging infrastructure, and supply dimensions

slides.title_text: Charging points growth by country — comparative analysis
slides.main_message: Charging point counts across 18 countries; comparative bar chart showing infrastructure maturity

slides.title_text: 04. Recommendations — section header
slides.main_message: Section divider: Recommendations on the way forward

slides.title_text: Recommendations for e-mobility players (1/2)
slides.main_message: First half of recommendations covering customer-facing improvements: simplify purchase journey including online, address used-EV battery SoH transparency, improve charging UX

slides.title_text: Recommendations for e-mobility players (2/2)
slides.main_message: Second half of recommendations covering infrastructure and product: invest in charging point reliability, target 6 EV prospect personas (Tech Enthusiasts, Dreamers, Pragmatic etc.), differentiated regional strategy

slides.title_text: Network contacts and closing
slides.main_message: Contacts and authors for the eReadiness 2023 Survey Report

provenance.input_json_source: PDF text extraction via web_fetch (no structural script run)
provenance.confidence_notes: AGGREGATED tagging — the actual report is ~81 pages but many are detail/data pages within section deep-dives. I aggregated logical units (e.g., 'EV Owners charging analyses' covers multiple actual pages). For production use, the full pptx version with the slide-tagger script would produce one entry per real slide. Structural fields unknown (no .pptx). HIGH confidence on deck-level tags and section structure. MEDIUM confidence on per-slide aggregation accuracy — actual report has more granular slides per topic than represented here. The 6-persona EV Prospects framework is a key consulting-style framework worth using as a Tier-3 template reference.
